witnesses.description: A three-observation two-dimensional rational sample with exact mean, centered rows, Gram matrix, and covariance matrix.

checks.claim: The listed sample has the stated mean vector.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes each mean coordinate exactly over rationals.

checks.claim: The listed centered rows equal each sample row minus the mean and sum to zero.
checks.notes: The validator checks row-by-row centering and zero column sums exactly.

checks.claim: The listed covariance matrix is the centered Gram matrix divided by the population denominator.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes (X - mu)^T (X - mu) and divides by the number of observations.

checks.claim: The fixed 2x2 covariance matrix has positive leading principal minors.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the two leading principal minors exactly; this is a fixed finite shadow, not a theorem about arbitrary covariance matrices.
The validator recomputes the covariance matrix and rejects the malformed covariance-entry claim; the separate qf-lra-bad-covariance-entry row owns the checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The fixed malformed covariance-entry claim is linearly inconsistent.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes the covariance entry as 4/9, while the malformed row claims 1/2; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: Statistical inference, PCA, asymptotic covariance theory, estimator consistency, and floating-point covariance algorithms are theorem/numerical-honesty work.

data.target_theorem_shape: For finite-dimensional random vectors, covariance matrices are positive semidefinite, support PCA/spectral decompositions under stated assumptions, and sample covariance estimators satisfy statistical guarantees only under explicit probabilistic hypotheses.
data.future_checker: Lean theorem statements for covariance PSD and PCA/spectral facts plus separate numerical-honesty artifacts for floating-point covariance implementations.

checks.notes: The finite exact row is a checked resource, not a theorem about arbitrary covariance matrices, inference procedures, asymptotic statistics, or floating-point algorithms.
